openmeter/app.purpose: Marketplace registry and runtime lifecycle for installed billing apps (Stripe, Sandbox, CustomInvoicing). Owns the App interface, AppFactory/RegistryItem self-registration pattern, install/uninstall flows, OAuth2, and per-customer data delegation — without hardcoding provider logic in the billing core.

patterns.name: AppFactory self-registration at constructor
patterns.description: Each app type's factory calls app.Service.RegisterMarketplaceListing inside its own New()/NewFactory(), not at wire time. The registry lives in-memory on the adapter.
patterns.example: stripe/service/factory.go: svc.appService.RegisterMarketplaceListing(RegistryItem{Listing: stripeListing, Factory: svc})

patterns.name: App embeds AppBase for shared identity methods
patterns.description: Concrete App structs (stripe.App, sandbox.App, custominvoicing.App) embed app.AppBase which provides GetAppBase(), GetID(), GetType(), GetStatus(), ValidateCapabilities(). Never duplicate these implementations.
patterns.example: type App struct { app.AppBase; ... }

patterns.name: Input.Validate() at every service entry point
patterns.description: Every service method calls input.Validate() before delegating to adapter or provider. Input structs enforce namespace cross-checks, required fields, and namespace equality via models.NewNillableGenericValidationError.
patterns.example: EnsureCustomerInput.Validate() checks AppID.Namespace == CustomerID.Namespace

patterns.name: models.Generic* error wrapping for all domain errors
patterns.description: Domain errors (AppNotFoundError, AppProviderError, etc.) embed models.NewGenericNotFoundError / GenericPreConditionFailedError so HTTP encoders map them to correct RFC 7807 status codes without special-casing.
patterns.example: errors.go: AppNotFoundError embeds models.NewGenericNotFoundError(...)

patterns.name: Compile-time interface assertion per file
patterns.description: Every file implementing an interface has a package-level var _ <Interface> = (*ConcreteType)(nil) assertion to catch drift at compile time.
patterns.example: var _ app.Service = (*service)(nil)

patterns.name: GetEventAppData() for type-neutral event serialization
patterns.description: App types implement GetEventAppData() (EventAppData, error) producing a map[string]any. Use NewEventApp(app) for serialization; consumers call ParseInto(&target). Never pass typed app structs directly to event payloads.
patterns.example: app/event.go: NewEventApp(app) calls app.GetEventAppData() to build EventApp payload

patterns.name: ServiceHooks[AppBase] for cross-domain lifecycle reactions
patterns.description: models.ServiceHookRegistry[AppBase] is embedded on app.Service; hooks fire at five points (PostCreate, PreUpdate, PostUpdate, PreDelete, PostDelete) inside the mutation transaction so a failing hook rolls back the DB write.
patterns.example: service/service.go: s.hooks.PostCreate(ctx, &appBase) inside transaction.Run

key_file_guides.role: Full public API: composes MarketplaceService, AppLifecycleService, CustomerDataService, and models.ServiceHooks[AppBase].
key_file_guides.watch_for: RegisterHooks and RegisterRequestValidator are separate — one for lifecycle hooks, one for pre-mutation validation. Both are needed when wiring cross-domain constraints.

key_file_guides.role: AppFactory, AppFactoryInstallWithAPIKey, AppFactoryInstall interfaces and RegistryItem — the extension point for new app types.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RegistryItem.Factory must be non-nil; RegistryItem.Validate() enforces this. A new app type needs both a Factory implementation and a registered MarketplaceListing.

key_file_guides.role: AppBase value type with shared fields (Type, Status, Listing, Metadata) and canonical GetAppBase/GetID/GetType/GetStatus/ValidateCapabilities implementations.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AppType constants must be kept in sync with Ent schema app_type enum. Do not add app-type-specific fields here.

key_file_guides.role: Typed domain errors: AppNotFoundError, AppDefaultNotFoundError, AppProviderAuthenticationError, AppProviderError, AppProviderPreConditionError, AppCustomerPreConditionError.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Each error type has an IsXxxError(err) helper. Always use these typed errors; never return raw fmt.Errorf from service or adapter methods.

key_file_guides.role: Domain event types (AppCreateEvent v1, AppUpdateEvent v2, AppDeleteEvent v2) and EventApp/EventAppData serialization helpers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AppUpdateEvent is v2 (carries full EventApp); AppCreateEvent is v1 (carries only AppBase). Do not bump version numbers in-place — add a new versioned struct.

anti_patterns: Calling adapter/Ent methods directly inside App method implementations — app-type logic must go through the service layer.
Returning errors without wrapping in a models.Generic* type — HTTP error encoder falls through to 500 for unrecognized types.
Constructing RegistryItem without setting Factory — RegistryItem.Validate() panics and the app cannot be installed.
Adding new AppType constants without updating AppType.Validate() and the Ent schema app_type enum — causes runtime panics on unknown type.
Publishing events inside the adapter layer — event publishing is exclusively the service layer's responsibility after successful adapter writes.

decisions.decision: Registry (marketplace listings) lives in-memory on the adapter, not the database.
decisions.rationale: Listings are static metadata provided by factories at startup; they don't need persistence and would add unnecessary DB round-trips for every install call.

decisions.decision: OAuth2 install methods on the adapter return 'not implemented' — only API-key and no-credentials installs are storage-backed.
decisions.rationale: OAuth2 flow state is transient and provider-specific; adapter-level storage would couple the adapter to provider protocols.

decisions.decision: No per-app-type advisory lock — multiple apps of the same AppType per namespace are supported by design.
decisions.rationale: The Ent schema index on (namespace, type) is non-unique; users legitimately run multiple Stripe region apps or sandbox apps in one namespace.

code_examples.scenario: Implementing a new app type factory that self-registers at construction time

openmeter/customer.purpose: Customer lifecycle management (CRUD, usage attributions, soft-delete) with two extension registries — RequestValidatorRegistry for pre-mutation cross-domain guards and ServiceHooks[Customer] for post-lifecycle callbacks — that avoid circular imports between billing, ledger, and subscription domains.

patterns.name: transaction.Run wraps every mutation
patterns.description: All CreateCustomer, UpdateCustomer, DeleteCustomer calls go through transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue in service/service.go, ensuring hooks and event publishes are atomic with the DB write.
patterns.example: service/customer.go: return transaction.Run(ctx, s.adapter, func(ctx context.Context, tx customer.Adapter) (*Customer, error) { ... })

patterns.name: RequestValidatorRegistry pre-mutation fan-out
patterns.description: Before any DB write, service calls s.requestValidatorRegistry.ValidateXxx(ctx, input) which fans out to all registered validators with errors.Join. Validators run before the transaction — they block the operation.
patterns.example: requestvalidator.go: requestValidatorRegistry.ValidateDeleteCustomer fans out to all registered validators

patterns.name: ServiceHooks[Customer] post-lifecycle fan-out inside the transaction
patterns.description: models.ServiceHooks[Customer] is embedded in Service; hooks fire at PostCreate/PostUpdate/PostDelete inside the mutation transaction. Hooks calling back into customer.Service must use NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) to prevent re-entrant invocations.
patterns.example: service/service.go: s.hooks.PostCreate(ctx, customer) after successful adapter write, inside transaction.Run

patterns.name: models.Generic* error types for all domain conditions
patterns.description: All customer-specific errors (KeyConflictError, SubjectKeyConflictError, UpdateAfterDeleteError) embed a models.Generic* error so HTTP encoders map them to the correct status code.
patterns.example: errors.go: KeyConflictError embeds models.NewGenericConflictError(...)

patterns.name: TransactingRepo wrapper on every adapter method
patterns.description: adapter/customer.go wraps every method body in entutils.TransactingRepo so the adapter rebinds to any in-flight transaction from ctx, or starts its own.
patterns.example: adapter/customer.go: entutils.TransactingRepo(ctx, a.db, func(ctx context.Context, tx *entdb.Tx) (*Customer, error) { ... })

patterns.name: Soft-delete pattern via DeletedAt
patterns.description: Customers are never hard-deleted. DeleteCustomer sets DeletedAt; queries default to excluding deleted records (IncludeDeleted=false). Customer.IsDeleted() checks DeletedAt before clock.Now().
patterns.example: customer.go: func (c Customer) IsDeleted() bool { return c.DeletedAt != nil && c.DeletedAt.Before(clock.Now()) }

key_file_guides.role: Service interface: composes CustomerService + RequestValidatorService + models.ServiceHooks[Customer]. Full API surface for the customer domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: RegisterHooks and RegisterRequestValidator are separate registries — one for lifecycle hooks, one for pre-mutation validation. Both are needed when wiring cross-domain constraints.

key_file_guides.role: Customer, CustomerMutate, CustomerID, CustomerUsageAttribution domain types and all input types (CreateCustomerInput, UpdateCustomerInput, ListCustomersInput, GetCustomerInput).
key_file_guides.watch_for: GetCustomerInput supports three lookup modes (by ID, by Key, by IDOrKey) — never accept a raw string; always construct one of the typed lookup structs. CustomerID is a type alias for models.NamespacedID.

key_file_guides.role: RequestValidator interface, NoopRequestValidator, and requestValidatorRegistry with RWMutex fan-out for pre-mutation blocking validation.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Register() is not idempotent — registering the same validator twice doubles validation calls. Wire providers must guard against double-registration.

key_file_guides.role: Typed domain errors: KeyConflictError, SubjectKeyConflictError, UpdateAfterDeleteError, ErrDeletingCustomerWithActiveSubscriptions (as ValidationIssue).
key_file_guides.watch_for: ErrDeletingCustomerWithActiveSubscriptions is a models.ValidationIssue (not a models.GenericError) and carries the list of active subscription IDs in attrs.

key_file_guides.role: Adapter interface: composes CustomerAdapter + entutils.TxCreator. The persistence boundary.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adapter never returns domain events — that is the service layer's responsibility. Adapter methods return typed domain objects (*Customer) not raw Ent rows.

anti_patterns: Calling customer.Service from inside a hook without NewContextWithSkipSubjectCustomer(ctx) — causes infinite re-entrant hook invocations.
Performing DB writes outside transaction.Run / transaction.RunWithNoValue in the service layer — partial writes are not rolled back on hook or publish failure.
Returning raw fmt.Errorf for domain conditions (not-found, conflict, validation) — HTTP encoders depend on models.Generic* typed errors.
Using context.Background() or context.TODO() in service or adapter methods — always propagate caller ctx.
Importing app/common in test files under customer/testutils — causes import cycles; build test deps from package constructors directly.

decisions.decision: RequestValidatorRegistry and ServiceHooks are separate registries with distinct call sites.
decisions.rationale: Pre-mutation validators run before any DB write and block the operation on failure; post-lifecycle hooks run after success and may have side-effects. Mixing them would lose the timing guarantee.

decisions.decision: Soft-delete rather than hard delete for customers and customer_subjects.
decisions.rationale: Billing, subscription, and entitlement records reference customer IDs and cannot be orphaned. Soft-delete preserves referential integrity while logically removing the customer.

code_examples.scenario: Registering a cross-domain pre-mutation validator that blocks customer deletion when billing records exist

openmeter/ingest.purpose: CloudEvent ingestion pipeline: the Collector interface receives single events and forwards them to Kafka; DeduplicatingCollector wraps any Collector with Redis or in-memory deduplication; ingestadapter decorates with OTel telemetry without altering ingest behavior.

patterns.name: Collector interface wrapping via struct embedding
patterns.description: DeduplicatingCollector embeds Collector and overrides Ingest() to call Deduplicator.IsUnique before delegating to the inner Collector. New decorators must follow the same embed-and-override pattern.
patterns.example: ingest/dedupe.go: DeduplicatingCollector struct embeds Collector and adds Deduplicator field; Ingest calls IsUnique before delegating

patterns.name: OTel metric instruments allocated in constructor, never in Ingest()
patterns.description: ingestadapter/telemetry.go creates Int64Counters in New(), not inside Ingest(). This avoids allocation and registration overhead on every event.
patterns.example: ingestadapter/telemetry.go: New() creates events_ingested and errors counters; Ingest() calls tracer.Start and records span

patterns.name: Topic provisioned on every Ingest call in kafkaingest
patterns.description: kafkaingest/collector.go calls TopicResolver.Resolve + TopicProvisioner.EnsureExists on each Ingest call, not only at namespace creation. Ensures topics exist even if the namespace was created without Kafka available.
patterns.example: kafkaingest/collector.go: Ingest calls resolver.Resolve then provisioner.EnsureExists before Producer.Produce

patterns.name: Content-type dispatch in httpdriver
patterns.description: httpdriver/ingest.go decodes requests by Content-Type: single (application/cloudevents+json) or batch (application/cloudevents-batch+json). Invalid content types return ErrorInvalidContentType, not raw errors.
patterns.example: httpdriver/ingest.go: switch on r.Header.Get('Content-Type') to select decoder path

patterns.name: KafkaProducerGroup as oklog/run pair, not an internal goroutine
patterns.description: kafkaingest returns a KafkaProducerGroup that must be added to the caller's run.Group to process delivery reports. Never start it as an internal goroutine inside NewCollector.
patterns.example: kafkaingest/collector.go: NewCollector returns (Collector, KafkaProducerGroup, error); caller adds group to run.Group

key_file_guides.role: Collector interface definition — the only required interface for the ingest domain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Close() is a lifecycle method; callers must call it to drain the Kafka producer before shutdown. Do not add new methods to Collector without considering all implementations.

key_file_guides.role: DeduplicatingCollector — thin deduplication wrapper over any Collector using dedupe.Deduplicator.
key_file_guides.watch_for: IsUnique returns (true, nil) for unique events and (false, nil) for duplicates. A (false, err) case means the deduplicator itself failed — the event is still forwarded to avoid data loss.

key_file_guides.role: ingest.Service wraps Collector with namespace-scoped validation and exposes IngestEvents for multi-event batches.
key_file_guides.watch_for: service.go validates namespace before calling Collector.Ingest. New HTTP endpoints that accept events must go through Service, not Collector directly.

anti_patterns: Calling Producer.Produce in kafkaingest without running KafkaProducerGroup in an oklog/run.Group — delivery errors are silently dropped.
Creating OTel metric instruments inside Ingest() instead of in the constructor — causes allocation and registration overhead per event.
Reading namespace from URL path params in httpdriver instead of NamespaceDecoder — bypasses static namespace injection for self-hosted deployments.
Adding new Kafka headers inside kafkaingest/collector.go without updating the sink worker's header parsing — breaks downstream deserialization.
Importing app/common from httpdriver — the HTTP handler must remain DI-agnostic.

decisions.decision: OTel telemetry is in a separate ingestadapter package rather than embedded in kafkaingest.
decisions.rationale: Keeps kafkaingest focused on Kafka protocol; ingestadapter can be swapped or omitted in tests without touching producer logic.

decisions.decision: Topic is resolved and provisioned on every Ingest call, not only at namespace creation.
decisions.rationale: Ensures topics exist even if the namespace was created without Kafka being available; removes a class of startup-order failures.

openmeter/ledger.purpose: Double-entry ledger for customer financial balances (FBO, Receivable, Accrued) and business accounts (Wash, Earnings, Brokerage). Transaction inputs are constructed exclusively via transactions.ResolveTransactions with typed template structs; noop/ provides zero-value implementations when credits.enabled=false.

patterns.name: TransactionInput constructed only via transaction templates
patterns.description: All ledger postings are created by calling transactions.ResolveTransactions with typed template structs. Raw EntryInput/TransactionInput construction outside the transactions/ package bypasses sub-account routing and routing rule validation.
patterns.example: transactions/resolve.go: ResolveTransactions iterates templates, calls resolve(), then calls ledger.CommitGroup

patterns.name: credits.enabled=false routes to noop/ implementations
patterns.description: app/common wires ledger.Ledger, ledger.AccountResolver, and ledgeraccount.Service to noop/ structs when credits.enabled=false. Every new ledger write path must have a corresponding noop path — enforce via compile-time interface assertions in noop/noop.go.
patterns.example: noop/noop.go: var _ ledger.Ledger = Ledger{}; var _ ledger.AccountResolver = AccountResolver{}

patterns.name: Route → canonical routing key via BuildRoutingKeyV1
patterns.description: SubAccount keys are derived from Route via ledger.BuildRoutingKeyV1(Route{...}). Never manually construct routing key strings — the canonical format normalizes decimals and nil fields.
patterns.example: routing.go: BuildRoutingKeyV1 produces 'currency:USD|tax_code:null|features:null|cost_basis:0.7|...'

patterns.name: BookTransaction always inside transaction.Run with ValidateTransactionInputWith
patterns.description: historical/ledger.go calls ValidateTransactionInputWith(ctx, tx, routingValidator) then repo.BookTransaction inside transaction.Run. Calling BookTransaction without validation or outside transaction.Run loses the account lock and routing invariants.
patterns.example: historical/ledger.go: transaction.Run(..., func(...) { ValidateTransactionInputWith; repo.BookTransaction })

patterns.name: ChargeAnnotations on every CommitGroup call
patterns.description: chargeadapter handlers annotate every ledger.CommitGroup call with ChargeAnnotations (charge ID, type, direction) via helpers. Omitting annotations breaks traceability and correction logic.
patterns.example: chargeadapter/annotations.go: ChargeAnnotations struct; helpers.go sets them on every CommitGroup input

patterns.name: Provisioning lock before EnsureCustomerAccounts
patterns.description: resolvers/account.go acquires a lockr.Locker advisory lock before EnsureCustomerAccounts to prevent duplicate account creation under concurrency. Lock uses a 5-second timeout.
patterns.example: resolvers/account.go: locker.LockForTX(ctx, lockKey) before CreateCustomerAccounts

key_file_guides.role: AccountType constants (FBO, Receivable, Accrued, Wash, Earnings, Brokerage), CustomerAccounts/BusinessAccounts structs, AccountResolver interface.
key_file_guides.watch_for: AccountResolver.GetCustomerAccounts panics for unprovisioned customers — always call EnsureCustomerAccounts first or check via resolvers.

key_file_guides.role: Typed account interfaces per account type (CustomerFBOAccount, CustomerReceivableAccount, CustomerAccruedAccount, BusinessAccount) with GetSubAccountForRoute signatures.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CustomerFBORouteParams requires CreditPriority (non-pointer int). CustomerReceivableRouteParams requires TransactionAuthorizationStatus. Missing required fields fail Validate().

key_file_guides.role: Route struct, BuildRoutingKeyV1, RoutingKey — the canonical hash key for sub-account routing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: CostBasis decimal is canonicalized by trimming trailing zeros. Always construct Route via typed field assignment, never string parsing.

key_file_guides.role: ValidateInvariance (debit-credit balance check), ValidateEntryInput, ValidateTransactionInputWith — called before every BookTransaction.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ValidateInvariance checks that all entry amounts sum to zero. Use alpacadecimal, not float64.

key_file_guides.role: Zero-value noop implementations of all ledger interfaces used when credits.enabled=false.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Every new ledger interface method must be added to the noop with a zero-value return. Missing methods break compile when credits.enabled=false.

anti_patterns: Constructing EntryInput/TransactionInput outside the transactions/ package — bypasses sub-account routing and routing rule validation.
Registering CustomerLedgerHook or namespace ledger handler when credits.enabled=false — these are real DB write paths; noop variants must be used instead.
Calling repo.BookTransaction outside a transaction.Run block — loses the account lock and validation atomicity.
Adding a new transaction template without registering it in transactionTemplateByName in correction.go — the template becomes uncorrectable.
Using context.Background() in any ledger method — breaks OTel tracing and transaction context propagation.

decisions.decision: Transaction templates (transactions/) are the only permitted construction site for EntryInput/TransactionInput.
decisions.rationale: Centralizing construction enforces routing rule compliance and makes corrections (reversals) traceable via annotation-stored template names.

decisions.decision: noop/ package provides compile-time-checked no-ops for all ledger interfaces.
decisions.rationale: Allows billing and customer domain to remain structurally identical regardless of credits.enabled flag — no nil checks needed in business logic.

decisions.decision: Tri-tuple cursor (bookedAt, createdAt, ID) for transaction pagination instead of auto-increment offset.
decisions.rationale: Ledger transactions are append-only but may be inserted out of clock order; a composite cursor with ID tie-breaking provides stable pagination.

code_examples.scenario: Posting a double-entry transaction via templates (the only permitted pattern)

openmeter/productcatalog.purpose: Root domain package for the product catalog: defines shared value types (RateCard, Price, Discount, EntitlementTemplate, EffectivePeriod, PlanAddon, Phase, ProRatingConfig) and validators consumed by all sub-packages (addon, plan, planaddon, feature, subscription). Acts as the shared type contract between billing, entitlement, and subscription layers.

patterns.name: ValidatorFunc[T] composition instead of inline Validate()
patterns.description: All domain types implement models.Validator and models.CustomValidator[T]; validation is composed from named ValidatorFunc[T] functions (ValidateAddonMeta(), ValidateAddonRateCards()) and called via models.Validate(). Publishable() adds stricter validators on top of the base set.
patterns.example: func (a Addon) Validate() error { return a.ValidateWith(ValidateAddonMeta(), ValidateAddonRateCards()) }

patterns.name: ValidationIssue sentinel errors with ErrCode constants
patterns.description: All domain errors are package-level models.ValidationIssue vars with ErrCodeXxx / ErrXxx pairs. HTTP status codes are attached via commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute. Never return plain fmt.Errorf at the package boundary.
patterns.example: var ErrRateCardFeatureNotFound = models.NewValidationIssue(ErrCodeRateCardFeatureNotFound, "feature not found", models.WithCriticalSeverity(), commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute(http.StatusBadRequest))

patterns.name: Discriminated union types with custom MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON
patterns.description: Polymorphic types (RateCard, Price, EntitlementTemplate) use unexported type-switch structs with explicit MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON. Always read the type discriminator field first, then unmarshal the concrete variant.
patterns.example: EntitlementTemplate.UnmarshalJSON reads serde.Type then delegates to MeteredEntitlementTemplate / StaticEntitlementTemplate / BooleanEntitlementTemplate

patterns.name: EffectivePeriod-based status derivation (no stored status field)
patterns.description: Status (Draft/Active/Archived/Invalid) is always computed from EffectivePeriod.EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo relative to clock.Now() or a supplied time — never stored as a separate field. Use StatusAt(t) for deterministic testing.
patterns.example: AddonMeta.Status() calls StatusAt(clock.Now()); zero EffectivePeriod = Draft, EffectiveFrom in past + no EffectiveTo = Active

patterns.name: RateCards collection helpers over direct slice iteration
patterns.description: RateCards ([]RateCard) exposes collection-level methods: SingleBillingCadence(), AsProductCatalogRateCards(), ValidateRateCards(). Use these helpers rather than ranging over the slice directly to preserve validation semantics.
patterns.example: ValidateAddonHasSingleBillingCadence checks a.RateCards.SingleBillingCadence() before publishing

patterns.name: models.ErrorWithFieldPrefix for structured error paths
patterns.description: Field-path context is attached via models.ErrorWithFieldPrefix(models.NewFieldSelectorGroup(models.NewFieldSelector("field")), err) so HTTP encoders can generate JSONPath selectors for client-side field highlighting.
patterns.example: models.ErrorWithFieldPrefix(models.NewFieldSelectorGroup(models.NewFieldSelector("ratecards").WithExpression(models.WildCard)), ErrRateCardMultipleBillingCadence)

key_file_guides.role: Single source of truth for all productcatalog-level ValidationIssue sentinel errors — every new validation error must be added here as an ErrCode const + var pair.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Forgetting commonhttp.WithHTTPStatusCodeAttribute on errors that need a non-500 HTTP mapping; using plain errors.New instead of models.NewValidationIssue.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the RateCard interface (Type, AsMeta, Key, Merge, Clone, Compatible, GetBillingCadence, IsBillable) and RateCardMeta.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding fields to RateCardMeta without updating Clone(); forgetting to add a case in the type-switch JSON serializer.

key_file_guides.role: Defines the Price discriminated union (FlatPrice, UnitPrice, TieredPrice, DynamicPrice, PackagePrice) with custom JSON and PaymentTermType enum.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new PriceType without updating the pricer interface, MarshalJSON/UnmarshalJSON switch, and all downstream adapters that map Price entities.

key_file_guides.role: Defines EntitlementTemplate discriminated union (Metered, Static, Boolean) with custom JSON. Used as a field of RateCardMeta.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Adding a new EntitlementType without updating all four switch statements in MarshalJSON, UnmarshalJSON, Validate, and Equal.

key_file_guides.role: Shared EffectivePeriod type embedded by Plan and Addon. Governs Draft/Active/Archived status transitions.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Setting EffectivePeriod fields directly to alter status — only Publish/Archive operations should mutate these fields.

anti_patterns: Returning plain fmt.Errorf or errors.New at the package boundary — always use models.NewValidationIssue sentinels or wrap with models.NewGenericValidationError.
Directly mutating EffectivePeriod.EffectiveFrom/EffectiveTo outside of Publish/Archive operations — status is derived, not stored.
Adding a new discriminated union variant (RateCardType, PriceType, EntitlementType) without updating MarshalJSON, UnmarshalJSON, Equal, Validate, and all downstream adapter type-switches.
Importing app/common or any DI package from this package — it must remain a pure domain types package.
Calling RateCard.Compatible() or RateCard.Merge() without first verifying Type() equality — mismatched types cause panics or silent data loss.

decisions.decision: ValidatorFunc[T] composition instead of inline Validate() logic.
decisions.rationale: Allows callers to run stricter validation (Publishable) by composing additional ValidatorFunc values on top of the base set, without duplicating logic or adding conditional branches inside Validate().

decisions.decision: EffectivePeriod-based status derivation with no stored status field.
decisions.rationale: Avoids status synchronization bugs: status is always consistent with the stored timestamps and requires no additional write when times are updated.

decisions.decision: Discriminated union types with unexported fields and custom JSON.
decisions.rationale: Prevents accidental construction of zero-valued union types; forces callers through typed constructors (NewEntitlementTemplateFrom, NewPriceFrom) that set the discriminator correctly.

code_examples.scenario: Adding a new ValidatorFunc and wiring it into Publishable validation
